_provenance: Claude-authored original passages (DeepSeek not used). CET-4 选词填空 style: ~260-word original passage, 10 numbered blanks, 15-word bank (10 answers + 5 distractors), answers = distinct bank indices, each blank grammatically + semantically unique.

sets.passage: A few years ago, many people predicted that public libraries would soon disappear. In the (1)____ age, when almost any book or article can be found online, why would anyone still visit a building full of paper? Yet libraries have not only managed to (2)____; in many cities they are busier than ever. One reason is that libraries provide free (3)____ to knowledge. Not everyone can afford a computer or a fast internet connection at home, and for these readers such free access is absolutely (4)____. At the library they can read newspapers and make use of learning (5)____ that would otherwise be far too (6)____ to buy. Libraries are also places where people (7)____. Students come to study together, parents bring their children to story hours, and retired people attend free lectures. In this way the library strengthens the local (8)____, giving people of all ages a shared space. Of course, the modern library has changed. Many now (9)____ e-books, music, and even tools, not just printed books. Some areas are kept in complete (10)____ for those who need to concentrate, while others welcome lively group work. Far from dying out, libraries are quietly reinventing themselves for a new century.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 access,1 community,2 digital,3 essential,4 expensive,5 gather,6 offer,7 resources,8 silence,9 survive,10 quietly,11 growth,12 rapidly,13 borrow,14 comfortable。(1) digital age=2；(2) managed to survive=9；(3) free access=0；(4) absolutely essential=3；(5) learning resources=7；(6) too expensive=4；(7) people gather=5；(8) local community=1；(9) now offer=6；(10) complete silence=8。干扰词: quietly/growth/rapidly/borrow/comfortable。

sets.passage: Ten years ago, working from home was unusual. Today, after a global health crisis forced millions to try it, remote work has become a normal and (1)____ option for many companies. The change has brought clear advantages, but also new (2)____. The biggest (3)____ is time. Without a daily (4)____ to the office, workers can save one or two hours every day, which many use to exercise, rest, or simply enjoy a slower morning. Research also suggests that some staff are actually more (5)____ at home, because they can avoid the constant (6)____ of a busy office. Employers gain as well. By allowing remote work, a company can (7)____ its search for talent far beyond its own city, hiring skilled (8)____ from anywhere in the country. At the same time, expensive office costs can be sharply (9)____. However, remote work is not perfect. Some people find it hard to (10)____ when family members are moving around them. The most successful companies therefore offer a mix of office and home days, keeping the freedom that workers value while protecting the human connections every team needs.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 challenges,1 flexible,2 commute,3 benefit,4 distractions,5 productive,6 employees,7 expand,8 concentrate,9 reduced,10 lonely,11 separate,12 balance,13 regularly,14 distance。(1) flexible option=1；(2) new challenges=0；(3) biggest benefit=3；(4) daily commute=2；(5) more productive=5；(6) constant distractions=4；(7) expand its search=7；(8) skilled employees=6；(9) sharply reduced=9；(10) hard to concentrate=8。干扰词: lonely/separate/balance/regularly/distance。
